REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (157 Shakespeare St., San Francisco, Ca 94112)
Off Calendar Dec-06-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Successor Trustee.

Ms. Ng presented an endorsed copy of the re-appraisal for sale. The sale was confirmed at $313,400.00 subject to the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $279,000.00 is required.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Jul-28-2010 Continued To Aug-25-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 291935 Conservatorship of Charles Joiner.

Appearances: Bruce Feder for Conservatee. Lillian Ng for Rena Harel.

Ms. Ng stated that there were several bank accounts in the Estate, that the joint accounts were not in the Conservator's name, that they were in the name of the Conservatee, the Son and the Stepson, that they did not have all of the bank statements as they were re-routed by Mr. Speech to Detroit and that she would request the bank statements in writing. In addition, Ms. Ng. stated that there was a temporary conservatorship of the estate and the person in Michigan, that they were working with the Public Guardian over there, that the Conservatee was living with the Son in Detroit, requested a 30 day continuance stating that the hearing date in Michigan was set for August 10, 2010 and that she did not have Counsel in Michigan. Mr. Feder stated that the Conservatee was settled over there, that the Conservatee did not think he would be moving back here, that it was appropriate to have the conservatorship transferred there and have someone appointed there, that the real property was here in the Trust and that the Court may need to retain jurisdiction over the Trust. Ms. Ng stated that bond was not set by the Examiner but that she would review the file to determine the bond amount. The matters were continued to August 25, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Jun-30-2010 Continued To Jul-28-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petn. For Court Supervision To Conservatee'S Trust, To Confirm Vacancy In The Office Of Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-23-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case no. 291935 (Conservatorship of Charles C. Joiner).

Appearances: Bruce Feder for and with Charles C. Joiner (Conservatee). Lillian Ng for and with Rena Harel (Temporary Conservator). Ballard Speech (Son).

Mr. Feder informed the Court that Mr. Joiner could travel if certain conditions were met regarding his medical needs and that Mr. Speech accompany him during travel. Ms. Ng and Ms. Harel presented an update for the Court. Discussions regarding medical insurance, possible parallel proceedings in Detroit, whether to appoint or keep the temp in place, and the trust issue were held. The Court granted the petition appointing Rena Harel as the Conservator of the Person and Estate of Charles C. Joiner (signing an order for the same in open court).

Further Orders (per written Order):
1. Compliance Status Hearing - August 5, 2009, 9:00 a.m., Dept. 204:
(a) filing of Proof of Service ("POS") of Order Appointing Conservator ("OAC"), Notice of Conservatee's Rights: 30 days from date of OAC;
(b) filing of General Plan: 60 days from date of OAC;
(c) filing of Inventory & Appraisal, Proof of Mailing of Inventory & Appraisal (or Order dispensing with same), service of Notice of How to Object to Inventory & Appraisal, and proof of recording of Letters (if the estate includes real property): 90 days from date of OAC.
2. Assessment Fee - $725 is charged but deferred.
3. First Account & Status Report - filed one week before June 30, 2010.
Bond is set at $596,000. Mr. Feder is to remain on two months regarding the transfer.

The Court granted the Petition for Court Supervision to the Conservatee's Trust, to Confirm Vacancy in the Office of Trustee in case no. 291936 (subject to review of the order).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Carol Harburda, CSR#8052. Court Investigator: Molly Glockler. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.

Petn. For Court Supervision To Conservatee'S Trust, To Confirm Vacancy In The Office Of Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-05-2009 Continued To Apr-23-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case no. 291935.
Appearances: Lillian Ng for and with Rena Harel. Bruce Feder for and with Charles Joiner. Ballard Speech (Son) in propria persona.

Ms. Ng stated that Mr. Joiner was under APS investigation, that he was self-neglecting, that he needs dialysis, that Ms. Harel had secured care for him, that she understood Mr. Speech wished to take his father to Detroit, but his father could not travel due to medical reasons. In addition, she stated in regard to the trust that they were marshalling assets, were trying to determine what was in the trust, and noted the home was in the trust. Mr. Feder stated that his client doesn't always know who Ms. Harel is, that he prefers people he knows manage his affairs, and with that in mind he did not want the appointment. In addition, Mr. Feder stated his client didn't know if he wanted to move to Detroit but he did want to visit to see if he liked it. Mr. Joiner stated he would like to move to Detroit because he has family there; no family is left in San Francisco. Mr. Speech stated that it was difficult to see his father like this and that he has things in motion in Detroit regarding his father's care. Ms. Harel expressed her concerns regarding the fragility of her client. Discussions were held regarding Mr. Joiner's ability to travel. The Court stated that Mr. Feder could get consent to speak with his client's doctors regarding his client traveling, and then find out what is set up in Detroit. The Court stated the temp was to remain in place (order signed in open court), that Ms. Harel is to talk with Mr. Joiner's doctor to make sure he can fly, and she is to check out the home in Detroit. Discussions regarding joint tenancy funds were held. The Court ordered that no person is to touch any joint tenancy funds or accounts; Ms. Harel is in charge of funds. Cont. to 4-23-09.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Rose Miller CSR#5041. Clerk: Jackie Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy K. Sandberg.

Petn. For Court Supervision To Conservatee'S Trust, To Confirm Vacancy In The Office Of Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-24-2008 Continued To Mar-05-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Ex Parte Petition For Court Supervision Of Conservatee'S Trust, For Suspension Of Powers Of Current Trustee, And For The Appointment Of Temporary Successor Trustee (Prob Code Sec 17004; 15642(A), (B)(2); 15643(E), And 15660[D])
Off Calendar Dec-24-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 291935.

Appearances: Lillian Ng for and with Rena Harel (Proposed Temporary Conservator and Proposed Temporary Successor Trustee). Linda Robertshaw of Adult Protective Services.

Ms. Ng stated that the Proposed Conservatee was not making his medical appointments including dialysis, that a family member reported his taxes weren't being paid, that he had a firearm in the house, and that Chris Trade stated that Ballard Speech was the natural son of the Proposed Conservatee. In addition, Ms. Ng reported that Mr. Ballard visits with his father, that at a Thanksgiving visit he stated he wanted to take his father to Detroit, and that Mr. Ballard was upset about the petition. The Court stated that it had read in Chris Trader's declaration that Ballard Speech was adopted by a family in Michigan, and that he may not be entitled to service but the Court wants to keep him in the loop (continue service). Ms. Robertshaw addressed the gun and stated it was believed that the Proposed Conservatee still had the 357 Magnum and hollowpoint bullets somewhere in his home. Ms. Harel stated that at the original visit she and Ms. Robertshaw were unable to to locate the gun because of the state of the house, that as advised she would contact Kelly Dunn at the police department to accompany her to retrieve the gun. Ms. Ng reported that Chris Trade was named as the first Successor Trustee, that he was willing to step down as nominated to allow a third party to step in, but not Ballard Speech. The Court granted the petition appointing Rena Harel as Temporary Conservator of the Person and Estate of Charles Joiner in 291935 and as Temporary Successor Trustee in 291936 (orders signed in open court), and continued the hearing on the general to March 5, 2009 to be heard with the trust matter. Any notice sent out should include the new date.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Jackie Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy.
